'''Lewis Bienek''' (born 11 April 1998) is an [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship|Betfred Championship]].

He has previously played for the [[London Broncos|Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]],<ref name="Capital punishment for Batley Bulldogs, downed 76-16">{{cite web|url=http://www.londonbroncosrl.com/news/capital-punishment-for-batley-bulldogs-downed-76-16|title=Capital punishment for Batley Bulldogs, downed 76-16|publisher=London Broncos|date=2016-08-14|accessdate=2016-09-16}}</ref> spending time on loan from [[London Broncos|London]] at [[Oxford Rugby League|Oxford]] in [[RFL League 1|Kingstone Press League 1]].<ref name="Oxford RL 24 - 18 Hemel Stags">{{cite web|url=http://www.oxfordrl.com/sportyresults/oxford-rl-vs-hemel-stags|title=Oxford RL 24 - 18 Hemel Stags|publisher=Oxford|date=2016-06-05|accessdate=2016-09-16|archive-date=17 September 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160917082037/http://www.oxfordrl.com/sportyresults/oxford-rl-vs-hemel-stags/|url-status=dead}}</ref> Bienek played for [[Hull FC]] in the [[Super League]], and spent time on loan from [[Hull F.C.|Hull]] at the London Broncos, [[Batley Bulldogs]] and the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]]. He has also played for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Bienek was born in [[Sidcup]], London, England.

==Playing career==
===London Broncos===
[[File:Lewis Bienek London Broncos.jpg|thumb|upright|Bienek warming up for the London Broncos ahead of his professional debut in 2016]]
Bienek started his professional career at the [[London Broncos]], with a short loan period at [[Oxford Rugby League|Oxford]].{{citation needed|date=March 2023}}

===Hull FC===
Bienek has spent the majority of his time at [[Hull FC]] out on loan to the [[London Broncos]], [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]], [[Batley Bulldogs|Batley]] & [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]].{{citation needed|date=March 2023}}

===Castleford Tigers===
It was announced on 24 November 2020 that Bienek had joined the [[Castleford Tigers]] on an initial one-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55063816|title=Lewis Bienek: Castleford Tigers sign Hull FC prop on one-year deal|website=BBC Sport|accessdate=15 Dec 2020}}</ref> He made his Castleford début, coming off the interchange bench, on 16 Apr 2021 ([[Super League XXVI results#Round 3|Round 3]]) in the 52-16 home victory over the [[Leigh Centurions]].

===London Broncos (re-join)===
On 15 October 2021, it was reported that he had signed for the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=15 Oct 2021|accessdate=16 Oct 2021|title=Duo return to London Broncos|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/duo-return-to-london-broncos/}}</ref>

On 15 October 2023, he played in the London Broncos upset [[Million Pound Game]] victory over [[Toulouse Olympique]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67105947|title=Championship Grand Final: Toulouse 14-18 London Broncos - Capital club back in Super League|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
